overall_justification: While the edition includes factual reporting on coffee prices and international news, the most prominent and detailed article on page 1 focuses on the "Convênio de Taubaté" mentioned in the summary.  The article's emphasis on the social benefits and the need to address the plight of poor and unfortunate children aligns with the Expansionist perspective, which prioritized the needs of productive sectors (like coffee) and aimed to use the Caixa de Conversão as a tool for broader economic improvement. The article implicitly connects the success of the Convênio (and by extension, the Caixa) with the well-being of children, framing it as a positive initiative.  This positive framing, combined with the article's length and placement, outweighs the neutral market reports.

supporting_evidence.quote: Applaudindo essa virgem iniciativa da Gazeta, tudo quanto tem feito em prol das crianças tem attestado a sympathia com que os seus bons costumes as teem attestado. Outras festas das festas do futuro. E essas festas, que não teem sido sem defeito, quanto ás suas bases, não teem sido sem o que ha de mais apreciável na correcção dos erros e das injustiças das condições de sorte.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This quote, from the prominent article on children and implicitly referencing the Convênio de Taubaté, frames the initiative and related efforts in a positive light, emphasizing social benefits and the correction of injustices, aligning with the Expansionist perspective.

supporting_evidence.quote: As crianças robustas, não é preciso dizel-o, são o resultado de um grande movimento em favor dellas, ou antes em favor de seu espirito, não devemos esquecer que ha crianças que não podem partilhar as doses de alegria que o jornalismo está proporcionando.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This quote further reinforces the connection between positive social outcomes (healthy children) and the "grande movimento" likely related to the Convênio and the Caixa, suggesting an endorsement of these initiatives and their potential to improve the lives of Brazilians, a key tenet of the Expansionist view.
